matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: SCN5A pathogenic channelopathy model (SCN5A arrhythmia-channelopathy model)
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests an SCN5A Nav1.5 thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to cardiac sodium current, conduction velocity, arrhythmia susceptibility, channel trafficking/gating, or clinical channelopathy review. Exact classification and phenotype are essential.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed rs1805124 (SCN5A H558R) dosage suggests this sample carries the SCN5A prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests an SCN5A Nav1.5 thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to cardiac sodium current, conduction velocity, arrhythmia susceptibility, channel trafficking/gating, or clinical channelopathy review. Exact classification and phenotype are essential. This specific marker is curated as SCN5A common channel-modifier marker. rs1805124 (SCN5A H558R) is curated as a SCN5A marker for Cardiac conduction and arrhythmia biology, Brugada/long-QT/conduction disease review context. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the cardiac voltage-gated sodium channel Nav1.5 gating, conduction velocity, depolarization, and arrhythmia biology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched rs1805124 (SCN5A H558R) at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the SCN5A prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Can modify or tag cardiac voltage-gated sodium channel Nav1.5 gating, conduction velocity, depolarization, and arrhythmia biology.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests an SCN5A Nav1.5 thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to cardiac sodium current, conduction velocity, arrhythmia susceptibility, channel trafficking/gating, or clinical channelopathy review. Exact classification and phenotype are essential. An observed SCN5A variant places this sample in the curated SCN5A research context. The local SCN5A bundle is cardiac-channelopathy oriented. SCN5A interpretation requires exact allele, ECG/phenotype, inheritance, ancestry, and expert variant classification. SCN5A variants can produce gain- or loss-of-function effects depending on channel gating, trafficking, and cellular context.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a SCN5A variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests an SCN5A Nav1.5 thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to cardiac sodium current, conduction velocity, arrhythmia susceptibility, channel trafficking/gating, or clinical channelopathy review. Exact classification and phenotype are essential. SCN5A methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a cardiac voltage-gated sodium channel Nav1.5 gating, conduction velocity, depolarization, and arrhythmia biology gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data. The local SCN5A bundle is cardiac-channelopathy oriented. SCN5A interpretation requires exact allele, ECG/phenotype, inheritance, ancestry, and expert variant classification. SCN5A variants can produce gain- or loss-of-function effects depending on channel gating, trafficking, and cellular context.
